affected_pattern: A cohort of customers shows 10x usage every first-hour-of-day for one week
root_cause: Daily reset window double-counting — after a metering service restart, the daily-counter reset job ran at both 00:00 and 00:05 UTC for a week, flushing the daily accumulator twice and shifting the excess into the paid bucket at the hour boundary.
supporting_evidence: Usage Spike RCA Playbook: Daily-Counter Reset Defect pattern
Reset-job log shows duplicate execution across 7 days
Spike confined to the 00:00–01:00 hour consistently
recommended_action: Remove the duplicate reset job and restore single 00:00 UTC schedule
Re-rate the week and refund the misattributed paid-bucket usage
Add a reset-job ledger so double executions are visible as metrics
Add a daily invariant test: accumulator resets exactly once per day
